revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 新城神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 長崎県壱岐市勝本町新城東触字尻井441番地 |位置 = |祭神 = 壱岐の守護代 平景隆、元寇で殉難した将兵、諸神 |社格 = |創建 = 昭和年（1948年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''壱岐神社'''（いきじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[平景隆]]公を主祭神とする。
== 概要 == [[1274年]]（文永11年）10月14日、[[元]]軍・[[高麗]]連合軍は、[[対馬]]に続き、[[壱岐島]]の西側に上陸。[[壱岐]]守護代・[[平景隆]]は100余騎で応戦したものの圧倒的兵力差の前に敗れ、翌15日、平景隆は[[樋詰城]]で自害する。[[文永の役]]で戦死した壱岐の守護代・平景隆の本陣であった樋詰城跡に新城神社はが鎮座する。祭神の平景隆は[[元寇]]文永の役の日本国危き時に、国家を衛るため勇敢に戦い命を捧げた。
祭神は、国難事変に際して神威を顕し、平時に於ては国家の平和を護り給ふ神々である。 壱岐島民は[[文永]]11年（[[1274年]]）以来、新城神社御創建の願い、明治19年（[[1886年]]）本殿の建設を実現した。
なお、『[[高麗史]]』[[金方慶伝]]には、壱岐島での戦闘の模様が以下のように記されている。
元軍が壱岐島に至ると、日本軍は岸上に陣を布いて待ち受けていた。高麗軍の将である朴之亮及び金方慶の娘婿の趙卞はこれを蹴散らすと、敗走する日本兵を追った。壱岐島の日本軍は降伏を願い出たが、日本兵を1,000余り討ち取った。
== 歴史 == *文永11年（1274年） 元軍・高麗連合軍により島民が殲滅される *明治19年（[[1886年]]）本殿建設
== 主な祭典 == *1月1日 元旦祭 *2月11日 [[建国]]記念祭 *12月31日 除夜祭
== 参考文献 == *新城神社御由緒書 ==交通== 壱岐芦辺港から車で10分
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.iki-brand.jp/info.php?bid=BA000128&key=新城神社&page_num=1 新城神社]
{{commonscat|Iki-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しんじょうしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:元寇]] {{shinto-stub}}

元軍が壱岐島に至ると、日本軍は岸上に陣を布いて待ち受けていた。高麗軍の将である朴之亮及び金方慶の娘婿の趙卞はこれを蹴散らすと、敗走する日本兵を追った。壱岐島の日本軍は降伏を願い出たが、日本兵を1,000余り討ち取った。

なお、『[[高麗史]]』[[金方慶伝]]には、壱岐島での戦闘の模様が以下のように記されている。
元軍が壱岐島に至ると、日本軍は岸上に陣を布いて待ち受けていた。高麗軍の将である朴之亮及び金方慶の娘婿の趙卞はこれを蹴散らすと、敗走する日本兵を追った。壱岐島の日本軍は降伏を願い出たが、日本兵を1,000余り討ち取った。
== 歴史 == *文永11年（1274年） 元軍・高麗連合軍により島民が殲滅される *明治19年（[[1886年]]）本殿建設
== 主な祭典 == *1月1日 元旦祭 *2月11日 [[建国]]記念祭 *12月31日 除夜祭
== 参考文献 == *新城神社御由緒書
==周辺史跡== *[[文永の役]]新城古戦場:この一帯は、第一回目の元寇である[[文永の役]]の最後の激戦地であり、惨殺された多くの壱岐島民の死体を埋めた千人塚がある。元寇殉国忠魂塔が中央に建ち、観音像、本来の[[千人塚]]の標石である自然石がある。また、案内看板もあり、元寇の悲惨さを忍ぶ人が訪れている。 *唐人原古戦場
==交通== 壱岐芦辺港から車で10分 ==関連項目== *[[元寇]] *[[壱岐神社]] *[[小茂田浜神社]] *[[少弐資時]] *[[元寇資料館]]

{{神社 |名称 = 新城神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 長崎県壱岐市勝本町新城東触字尻井441番地 |位置 = |祭神 = 壱岐の守護代 平景隆、元寇で殉難した将兵、諸神 |社格 = |創建 = 昭和年（1948年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''新城神社'''（しんじょうじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。

== 概要 == [[1274年]]（文永11年）10月14日、[[元]]軍・[[高麗]]連合軍は、[[対馬]]に続き、[[壱岐島]]の西側に上陸。[[壱岐]]守護代・[[平景隆]]は100余騎で応戦したものの圧倒的兵力差の前に敗れ、翌15日、平景隆は[[樋詰城]]で自害する。[[文永の役]]で戦死した壱岐の守護代・平景隆の本陣であった樋詰城跡に新城神社はが鎮座する。祭神の平景隆は[[元寇]]文永の役の日本国危き時に、国家を衛るため勇敢に戦い命を捧げた。

なお、『[[高麗史]]』[[金方慶伝]]には、壱岐島での戦闘の模様が以下のように記されている。
元軍が壱岐島に至ると、日本軍は岸上に陣を布いて待ち受けていた。高麗軍の将である朴之亮及び金方慶の娘婿の趙卞はこれを蹴散らすと、敗走する日本兵を追った。壱岐島の日本軍は降伏を願い出たが、日本兵を1,000余り討ち取った。
== 歴史 == *文永11年（1274年） 元軍・高麗連合軍により島民が殲滅される *明治19年（[[1886年]]）本殿建設
== 主な祭典 == *1月1日 元旦祭 *2月11日 [[建国]]記念祭 *12月31日 除夜祭
== 参考文献 == *新城神社御由緒書
==周辺史跡== *[[文永の役]]新城古戦場:この一帯は、第一回目の元寇である[[文永の役]](1274年）の最後の激戦地であり、惨殺された多くの壱岐島民の死体を埋めた千人塚がある。元寇殉国忠魂塔が中央に建ち、観音像、本来の[[千人塚]]の標石である自然石がある。また、案内看板もあり、元寇の悲惨さを忍ぶことができる。
*[[少弐資時]]の墓:二度目の襲来である[[弘安の役]]では、[[蒙古]]・[[高麗]]連合の東路軍が[[対馬]]を襲い、次いで壱岐に攻め込んで来た([[1281年]])。元・高麗連合軍を迎え撃った当時の壱岐守護代少貳資時は、[[船匿城]](ふなかくしじょう)で全滅した。壱岐神社近くの少貳公園の松林の中に資時の墓があり、周辺には、元寇の戦艦と思われる[[碇]](いかり)石、急を知らせる防人の[[狼煙]](のろし)台やがある。
*唐人原古戦場

{{神社 |名称 = 新城神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 長崎県壱岐市勝本町新城東触字尻井441番地 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|49|59.7|N|129|43|5.5|E|region:JP-42_scale:10000|display=inline,title|name=新城神社}} |祭神 = 壱岐の守護代 平景隆、元寇で殉難した将兵、諸神 |社格 = |創建 = 昭和年（1948年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''新城神社'''（しんじょうじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[平景隆]]公を主祭神とする。
== 概要 == [[1274年]]（文永11年）10月14日、[[元]]軍・[[高麗]]連合軍は、[[対馬]]に続き、[[壱岐島]]の西側に上陸。[[壱岐]]守護代・[[平景隆]]は100余騎で応戦したものの圧倒的兵力差の前に敗れ、翌15日、平景隆は[[樋詰城]]で自害する。[[文永の役]]で戦死した壱岐の守護代・平景隆の本陣であった樋詰城跡に新城神社はが鎮座する。祭神の平景隆は[[元寇]]文永の役の日本国危き時に、国家を衛るため勇敢に戦い命を捧げた。
祭神は、国難事変に際して神威を顕し、平時に於ては国家の平和を護り給ふ神々である。 壱岐島民は[[文永]]11年（[[1274年]]）以来、新城神社御創建の願い、明治19年（[[1886年]]）本殿の建設を実現した。
なお、『[[高麗史]]』[[金方慶伝]]には、壱岐島での戦闘の模様が以下のように記されている。
元軍が壱岐島に至ると、日本軍は岸上に陣を布いて待ち受けていた。高麗軍の将である朴之亮及び金方慶の娘婿の趙卞はこれを蹴散らすと、敗走する日本兵を追った。壱岐島の日本軍は降伏を願い出たが、日本兵を1,000余り討ち取った。
== 歴史 == *文永11年（1274年） 元軍・高麗連合軍により島民が殲滅される *明治19年（[[1886年]]）本殿建設
== 主な祭典 == *1月1日 元旦祭 *2月11日 [[建国]]記念祭 *12月31日 除夜祭
== 参考文献 == *新城神社御由緒書
==周辺史跡== *[[文永の役]]新城古戦場:この一帯は、第一回目の元寇である[[文永の役]](1274年）の最後の激戦地であり、惨殺された多くの壱岐島民の死体を埋めた千人塚がある。元寇殉国忠魂塔が中央に建ち、観音像、本来の[[千人塚]]の標石である自然石がある。また、案内看板もあり、元寇の悲惨さを忍ぶことができる。
*[[少弐資時]]の墓:二度目の襲来である[[弘安の役]]では、[[蒙古]]・[[高麗]]連合の東路軍が[[対馬]]を襲い、次いで壱岐に攻め込んで来た([[1281年]])。元・高麗連合軍を迎え撃った当時の壱岐守護代少貳資時は、[[船匿城]](ふなかくしじょう)で全滅した。壱岐神社近くの少貳公園の松林の中に資時の墓があり、周辺には、元寇の戦艦と思われる[[碇]](いかり)石、急を知らせる防人の[[狼煙]](のろし)台やがある。
*唐人原古戦場
==交通== 壱岐芦辺港から車で10分 ==関連項目== *[[元寇]] *[[壱岐神社]] *[[小茂田浜神社]] *[[少弐資時]] *[[元寇資料館]]

*[[少弐資時]]の墓:二度目の襲来である[[弘安の役]]では、[[蒙古]]・[[高麗]]連合の東路軍が[[対馬]]を襲い、次いで壱岐に攻め込んで来た([[1281年]])。元・高麗連合軍を迎え撃った当時の壱岐守護代少貳資時は、[[船匿城]](ふなかくしじょう)で全滅した。壱岐神社近くの少貳公園の松林の中に資時の墓があり、周辺には、元寇の戦艦と思われる[[碇]](いかり)石、急を知らせる防人の[[狼煙]](のろし)台やがある。
*唐人原古戦場
==交通== 壱岐芦辺港から車で10分

{{神社 |名称 = 新城神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=長崎県}} |所在地 = 長崎県壱岐市勝本町新城東触字尻井441番地 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|49|59.7|N|129|43|5.5|E|region:JP-42_scale:10000|display=inline,title|name=新城神社}} |祭神 = 壱岐の守護代 平景隆、元寇で殉難した将兵、諸神 |社格 = |創建 = 昭和年（1948年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}
'''新城神社'''（しんじょうじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[平景隆]]公を主祭神とする。
== 概要 == [[1274年]]（文永11年）10月14日、[[元 (王朝)|元]]軍・[[高麗]]連合軍は、[[対馬]]に続き、[[壱岐島]]の西側に上陸。[[壱岐]]守護代・[[平景隆]]は100余騎で応戦したものの圧倒的兵力差の前に敗れ、翌15日、平景隆は[[樋詰城]]で自害する。[[文永の役]]で戦死した壱岐の守護代・平景隆の本陣であった樋詰城跡に新城神社はが鎮座する。祭神の平景隆は[[元寇]]文永の役の日本国危き時に、国家を衛るため勇敢に戦い命を捧げた。
祭神は、国難事変に際して神威を顕し、平時に於ては国家の平和を護り給ふ神々である。 壱岐島民は[[文永]]11年（[[1274年]]）以来、新城神社御創建の願い、明治19年（[[1886年]]）本殿の建設を実現した。
なお、『[[高麗史]]』[[金方慶伝]]には、壱岐島での戦闘の模様が以下のように記されている。
元軍が壱岐島に至ると、日本軍は岸上に陣を布いて待ち受けていた。高麗軍の将である朴之亮及び金方慶の娘婿の趙卞はこれを蹴散らすと、敗走する日本兵を追った。壱岐島の日本軍は降伏を願い出たが、日本兵を1,000余り討ち取った。
== 歴史 == *文永11年（1274年） 元軍・高麗連合軍により島民が殲滅される *明治19年（[[1886年]]）本殿建設
== 主な祭典 == *1月1日 元旦祭 *2月11日 [[建国]]記念祭 *12月31日 除夜祭
== 参考文献 == *新城神社御由緒書
==周辺史跡== *[[文永の役]]新城古戦場:この一帯は、第一回目の元寇である[[文永の役]](1274年）の最後の激戦地であり、惨殺された多くの壱岐島民の死体を埋めた千人塚がある。元寇殉国忠魂塔が中央に建ち、観音像、本来の[[千人塚]]の標石である自然石がある。また、案内看板もあり、元寇の悲惨さを忍ぶことができる。
*[[少弐資時]]の墓:二度目の襲来である[[弘安の役]]では、[[蒙古]]・[[高麗]]連合の東路軍が[[対馬]]を襲い、次いで壱岐に攻め込んで来た([[1281年]])。元・高麗連合軍を迎え撃った当時の壱岐守護代少貳資時は、[[船匿城]](ふなかくしじょう)で全滅した。壱岐神社近くの少貳公園の松林の中に資時の墓があり、周辺には、元寇の戦艦と思われる[[碇]](いかり)石、急を知らせる防人の[[狼煙]](のろし)台やがある。
*唐人原古戦場
==交通== 壱岐芦辺港から車で10分

== 祭神 == [[平景隆]]公を主祭神とする。
== 概要 == [[1274年]]（文永11年）10月14日、[[元 (王朝)|元]]軍・[[高麗]]連合軍は、[[対馬]]に続き、[[壱岐島]]の西側に上陸。[[壱岐]]守護代・[[平景隆]]は100余騎で応戦したものの圧倒的兵力差の前に敗れ、翌15日、平景隆は[[樋詰城]]で自害する。[[文永の役]]で戦死した壱岐の守護代・平景隆の本陣であった樋詰城跡に新城神社はが鎮座する。祭神の平景隆は[[元寇]]文永の役の日本国危き時に、国家を衛るため勇敢に戦い命を捧げた。
祭神は、国難事変に際して神威を顕し、平時に於ては国家の平和を護り給う神々である。 壱岐島民は[[文永]]11年（[[1274年]]）以来、新城神社御創建の願い、明治19年（[[1886年]]）本殿の建設を実現した。
なお、『[[高麗史]]』[[金方慶伝]]には、壱岐島での戦闘の模様が以下のように記されている。
元軍が壱岐島に至ると、日本軍は岸上に陣を布いて待ち受けていた。高麗軍の将である朴之亮及び金方慶の娘婿の趙卞はこれを蹴散らすと、敗走する日本兵を追った。壱岐島の日本軍は降伏を願い出たが、日本兵を1,000余り討ち取った。
== 歴史 == *文永11年（1274年） 元（モンゴル）・高麗（韓国）連合軍による島民大虐殺 *明治19年（[[1886年]]）本殿建設 [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|元寇の合戦の場となった壱岐の新城神社の神殿内部]] == 主な祭典 == *1月1日 元旦祭 *2月11日 [[建国]]記念祭 *12月31日 除夜祭
== 参考文献 == *新城神社御由緒書
==周辺史跡== *[[文永の役]]新城古戦場:この一帯は、第一回目の元寇である[[文永の役]](1274年）の最後の激戦地であり、惨殺された多くの壱岐島民の死体を埋めた千人塚がある。元寇殉国忠魂塔が中央に建ち、観音像、本来の[[千人塚]]の標石である自然石がある。また、案内看板もあり、元寇の悲惨さを忍ぶことができる。
*[[少弐資時]]の墓:二度目の襲来である[[弘安の役]]では、[[蒙古]]・[[高麗]]連合の東路軍が[[対馬]]を襲い、次いで壱岐に攻め込んで来た([[1281年]])。元・高麗連合軍を迎え撃った当時の壱岐守護代少貳資時は、[[船匿城]](ふなかくしじょう)で全滅した。壱岐神社近くの少貳公園の松林の中に資時の墓があり、周辺には、元寇の戦艦と思われる[[碇]](いかり)石、急を知らせる防人の[[狼煙]](のろし)台やがある。
*唐人原古戦場
==交通== 壱岐芦辺港から車で10分
==関連項目== *[[元寇]] *[[壱岐神社]] *[[小茂田浜神社]] *[[少弐資時]] *[[元寇資料館]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.iki-brand.jp/info.php?bid=BA000128&key=新城神社&page_num=1 新城神社]

== 歴史 == *文永11年（1274年） 元（モンゴル）・高麗（韓国）連合軍による島民大虐殺 *明治19年（[[1886年]]）本殿建設 [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|元寇の合戦の場となった壱岐の新城神社の神殿内部]] == 主な祭典 == *1月1日 元旦祭 *2月11日 [[建国]]記念祭 *12月31日 除夜祭
== 参考文献 == *新城神社御由緒書
==周辺史跡== *[[文永の役]]新城古戦場:この一帯は、第一回目の元寇である[[文永の役]](1274年）の最後の激戦地であり、惨殺された多くの壱岐島民の死体を埋めた千人塚がある。元寇殉国忠魂塔が中央に建ち、観音像、本来の[[千人塚]]の標石である自然石がある。また、案内看板もあり、元寇の悲惨さを忍ぶことができる。
*[[少弐資時]]の墓:二度目の襲来である[[弘安の役]]では、[[蒙古]]・[[高麗]]連合の東路軍が[[対馬]]を襲い、次いで壱岐に攻め込んで来た([[1281年]])。元・高麗連合軍を迎え撃った当時の壱岐守護代少貳資時は、[[船匿城]](ふなかくしじょう)で全滅した。壱岐神社近くの少貳公園の松林の中に資時の墓があり、周辺には、元寇の戦艦と思われる[[碇]](いかり)石、急を知らせる防人の[[狼煙]](のろし)台やがある。
*唐人原古戦場
==交通== 壱岐芦辺港から車で10分

{{神社 |名称 = 新城神社 |画像 = [[File:壱岐 新城神社鳥居.JPG|center|270px|元寇の合戦の場となった壱岐の新城神社の鳥居から本殿]] |所在地 = 長崎県壱岐市勝本町新城東触字尻井441番地 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|49|59.7|N|129|43|5.5|E|region:JP-42_scale:10000|display=inline,title|name=新城神社}} |地図 = Japan Nagasaki |祭神 = 壱岐の守護代 平景隆、元寇で殉難した将兵、諸神 |社格 = |創建 = 昭和年（1948年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}
'''新城神社'''（しんじょうじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[平景隆]]公を主祭神とする。
== 概要 == [[1274年]]（文永11年）10月14日、[[元 (王朝)|元]]軍・[[高麗]]連合軍は、[[対馬]]に続き、[[壱岐島]]の西側に上陸。[[壱岐]]守護代・[[平景隆]]は100余騎で応戦したものの圧倒的兵力差の前に敗れ、翌15日、平景隆は[[樋詰城]]で自害する。[[文永の役]]で戦死した壱岐の守護代・平景隆の本陣であった樋詰城跡に新城神社は鎮座する。祭神の平景隆は[[元寇]]文永の役の日本国危き時に、国家を衛るため勇敢に戦い命を捧げた。
祭神は、国難事変に際して神威を顕し、平時に於ては国家の平和を護り給う神々である。 壱岐島民は[[文永]]11年（[[1274年]]）以来、新城神社御創建の願い、明治19年（[[1886年]]）本殿の建設を実現した。
なお、『[[高麗史]]』[[金方慶伝]]には、壱岐島での戦闘の模様が以下のように記されている。
元軍が壱岐島に至ると、日本軍は岸上に陣を布いて待ち受けていた。高麗軍の将である朴之亮及び金方慶の娘婿の趙卞はこれを蹴散らすと、敗走する日本兵を追った。壱岐島の日本軍は降伏を願い出たが、日本兵を1,000余り討ち取った。
== 歴史 == *文永11年（1274年） 元（モンゴル）・高麗（韓国）連合軍による島民大虐殺 *明治19年（[[1886年]]）本殿建設 [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|元寇の合戦の場となった壱岐の新城神社の神殿内部]] == 主な祭典 == *1月1日 元旦祭 *2月11日 [[建国]]記念祭 *12月31日 除夜祭
== 参考文献 == *新城神社御由緒書

*[[少弐資時]]の墓:二度目の襲来である[[弘安の役]]では、[[蒙古]]・[[高麗]]連合の東路軍が[[対馬]]を襲い、次いで壱岐に攻め込んで来た([[1281年]])。元・高麗連合軍を迎え撃った当時の壱岐守護代少貳資時は、[[船匿城]](ふなかくしじょう)で全滅した。壱岐神社近くの少貳公園の松林の中に資時の墓があり、周辺には、元寇の戦艦と思われる[[碇]](いかり)石、急を知らせる防人の[[狼煙]](のろし)台やがある。
*唐人原古戦場

==周辺史跡== *[[文永の役]]新城古戦場:この一帯は、第一回目の元寇である[[文永の役]](1274年）の最後の激戦地であり、惨殺された多くの壱岐島民の死体を埋めた千人塚がある。元寇殉国忠魂塔が中央に建ち、観音像、本来の[[千人塚]]の標石である自然石がある。また、案内看板もあり、元寇の悲惨さを忍ぶことができる。 *唐人原古戦場 *高麗橋古戦場
==関連史跡== *[[少弐資時]]の墓:二度目の襲来である[[弘安の役]]では、[[蒙古]]・[[高麗]]連合の東路軍が[[対馬]]を襲い、次いで壱岐に攻め込んで来た([[1281年]])。元・高麗連合軍を迎え撃った当時の壱岐守護代少貳資時は、[[船匿城]](ふなかくしじょう)で全滅した。壱岐神社近くの少貳公園の松林の中に資時の墓があり、周辺には、元寇の戦艦と思われる[[碇]](いかり)石、急を知らせる防人の[[狼煙]](のろし)台やがある。
==交通== 壱岐芦辺港から車で10分

{{神社 |名称 = 新城神社 |画像 = [[File:壱岐 新城神社鳥居.JPG|center|270px|元寇の合戦の場となった壱岐の新城神社の鳥居から本殿]] |所在地 = 長崎県壱岐市勝本町新城東触字尻井441番地 | 緯度度 = 33 | 緯度分 = 49 | 緯度秒 = 59.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 129 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 5.5 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP-42 |地図 = Japan Nagasaki |祭神 = 壱岐の守護代 平景隆、元寇で殉難した将兵、諸神 |社格 = |創建 = 昭和年（1948年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|元寇の合戦の場となった壱岐の新城神社の神殿内部|200px]] [[File:Monument_of_Taira_Kagetaka's_Suicide_in_Shinjo_Shrine_on_Iki_Island.jpg|thumb|200px|新城神社内に設置されている平景隆自刃の地を表す碑]] [[File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|thumb|200px|新城古戦場・千人塚]] [[File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|thumb|200px|唐人原古戦場]] [[File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|thumb|200px|高麗橋古戦場]] '''新城神社'''（しんじょうじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[平景隆]]公を主祭神とする。
== 概要 == [[1274年]]（文永11年）10月14日、[[元 (王朝)|元]]軍・[[高麗]]連合軍は、[[対馬]]に続き、[[壱岐島]]の西側に上陸。[[壱岐]]守護代・[[平景隆]]は100余騎で応戦したものの圧倒的兵力差の前に敗れ、翌15日、平景隆は[[樋詰城]]で自害する。[[文永の役]]で戦死した壱岐の守護代・平景隆の本陣であった樋詰城跡に新城神社は鎮座する。祭神の平景隆は[[元寇]]文永の役の日本国危き時に、国家を衛るため勇敢に戦い命を捧げた。
祭神は、国難事変に際して神威を顕し、平時に於ては国家の平和を護り給う神々である。 壱岐島民は[[文永]]11年（[[1274年]]）以来、新城神社御創建の願い、明治19年（[[1886年]]）本殿の建設を実現した。
なお、『[[高麗史]]』[[金方慶伝]]には、壱岐島での戦闘の模様が以下のように記されている。
元軍が壱岐島に至ると、日本軍は岸上に陣を布いて待ち受けていた。高麗軍の将である朴之亮及び金方慶の娘婿の趙卞はこれを蹴散らすと、敗走する日本兵を追った。壱岐島の日本軍は降伏を願い出たが、日本兵を1,000余り討ち取った。
== 歴史 == *文永11年（1274年） - 元（モンゴル）・高麗（韓国）連合軍による島民大虐殺 *明治19年（[[1886年]]）- 本殿建設
== 主な祭典 == *1月1日 - 元旦祭 *2月11日 - [[建国]]記念祭 *12月31日 - 除夜祭
==周辺史跡== *[[文永の役]]新城古戦場（壱岐市勝本町新城東触1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 文永の役新城古戦場]（壱岐の名所情報）- [[壱岐市立一支国博物館]]ウェブサイト</ref>） :この一帯は、第一回目の元寇である[[文永の役]](1274年）の最後の激戦地であり、惨殺された多くの壱岐島民の死体を埋めた千人塚がある。元寇殉国忠魂塔が中央に建ち、観音像、本来の[[千人塚]]の標石である自然石がある。また、案内看板もあり、元寇の悲惨さを忍ぶことができる。 *唐人原古戦場（壱岐市勝本町北触<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 文永の役唐人原古戦場]（壱岐の名所情報）- 壱岐市立一支国博物館ウェブサイト</ref>） *高麗橋古戦場（壱岐市勝本町西戸触<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 文永の役高麗橋古戦場]（壱岐の名所情報）- 壱岐市立一支国博物館ウェブサイト</ref>）

{{神社 |名称 = 新城神社 |画像 = [[File:壱岐 新城神社鳥居.JPG|center|270px|元寇の合戦の場となった壱岐の新城神社の鳥居から本殿]] |所在地 = 長崎県壱岐市勝本町新城東触字尻井441番地 | 緯度度 = 33 | 緯度分 = 49 | 緯度秒 = 59.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 129 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 5.5 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP-42 |地図 = Japan Nagasaki |祭神 = 壱岐の守護代 平景隆、元寇で殉難した将兵、諸神 |社格 = |創建 = 昭和年（1948年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|元寇の合戦の場となった壱岐の新城神社の神殿内部|200px]] [[File:Monument_of_Taira_Kagetaka's_Suicide_in_Shinjo_Shrine_on_Iki_Island.jpg|thumb|200px|新城神社内に設置されている平景隆自刃の地を表す碑]]
'''新城神社'''（しんじょうじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[平景隆]]公を主祭神とする。
== 概要 == [[1274年]]（文永11年）10月14日、[[元 (王朝)|元]]軍・[[高麗]]連合軍は、[[対馬]]に続き、[[壱岐島]]の西側に上陸。[[壱岐]]守護代・[[平景隆]]は100余騎で応戦したものの圧倒的兵力差の前に敗れ、翌15日、平景隆は[[樋詰城]]で自害する。[[文永の役]]で戦死した壱岐の守護代・平景隆の本陣であった樋詰城跡に新城神社は鎮座する。祭神の平景隆は[[元寇]]文永の役の日本国危き時に、国家を衛るため勇敢に戦い命を捧げた。
祭神は、国難事変に際して神威を顕し、平時に於ては国家の平和を護り給う神々である。 壱岐島民は[[文永]]11年（[[1274年]]）以来、新城神社御創建の願い、明治19年（[[1886年]]）本殿の建設を実現した。
なお、『[[高麗史]]』[[金方慶伝]]には、壱岐島での戦闘の模様が以下のように記されている。
{{quotation|元軍が壱岐島に至ると、日本軍は岸上に陣を布いて待ち受けていた。高麗軍の将である朴之亮及び金方慶の娘婿の趙卞はこれを蹴散らすと、敗走する日本兵を追った。壱岐島の日本軍は降伏を願い出たが、日本兵を1,000余り討ち取った。}}
== 歴史 == *文永11年（1274年） - 元（モンゴル）・高麗（韓国）連合軍による島民大虐殺 *明治19年（[[1886年]]）- 本殿建設
== 主な祭典 == *1月1日 - 元旦祭 *2月11日 - [[建国]]記念祭 *12月31日 - 除夜祭
==周辺史跡== *[[文永の役]]新城古戦場（壱岐市勝本町新城東触1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 文永の役新城古戦場]（壱岐の名所情報）- [[壱岐市立一支国博物館]]ウェブサイト</ref>） :この一帯は、第一回目の元寇である[[文永の役]](1274年）の最後の激戦地であり、惨殺された多くの壱岐島民の死体を埋めた千人塚がある。元寇殉国忠魂塔が中央に建ち、観音像、本来の[[千人塚]]の標石である自然石がある。また、案内看板もあり、元寇の悲惨さを忍ぶことができる。 *唐人原古戦場（壱岐市勝本町北触<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 文永の役唐人原古戦場]（壱岐の名所情報）- 壱岐市立一支国博物館ウェブサイト</ref>） *高麗橋古戦場（壱岐市勝本町西戸触<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 文永の役高麗橋古戦場]（壱岐の名所情報）- 壱岐市立一支国博物館ウェブサイト</ref>） *文永の役 元軍上陸地（壱岐市勝本町本宮仲触 浦海（うろみ）漁港） <gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|新城古戦場 File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|唐人原古戦場 File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|高麗橋古戦場 File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|元軍上陸地（浦海） File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|浦海漁港風景 </gallery>
==交通== ;最寄りのバス停 *[[壱岐交通]] 「新城橋」バス停 ;最寄りの地方道 *[[長崎県道23号勝本石田線]] *[[芦辺漁港|芦辺港]]から車で10分
== 参考文献 == *新城神社御由緒書
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}{{脚注ヘルプ}}
==関連項目== *[[元寇]] *[[壱岐神社]] *[[壱岐市立一支国博物館]] *[[小茂田浜神社]]（[[対馬市]]厳原町小茂田） *[[少弐資時]] *[[元寇資料館]]（[[福岡県]]）
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.iki-brand.jp/info.php?bid=BA000128&key=新城神社&page_num=1 新城神社]

{{Other uses|Shinjō Shrine in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|the shrine formerly located in Taiwan|Shinjō Shrine (Taiwan)}} {{Shrine |Name = Shinjō Shrine |Image = [[File:壱岐 新城神社鳥居.JPG|center|270px|Torii and main hall of Shinjō Shrine in Iki, the site of the Mongol invasions]] |Location = Shinjō Higashifure 441, Katsumoto Town, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 49 | Latitude seconds = 59.7 | N or S = N |Longitude degrees = 129 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 5.5 | E or W = E |Map country code = JP-42 |Map = Japan Nagasaki |Deity = Taira Kagetaka, soldiers who fell during the Mongol invasions, various other deities |Type = |Established = 1948 |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Sacred sites = |Annual festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|Interior of the main hall at Shinjō Shrine|200px]] [[File:Monument_of_Taira_Kagetaka's_Suicide_in_Shinjo_Shrine_on_Iki_Island.jpg|thumb|200px|Monument marking the site of Taira Kagetaka’s suicide within Shinjō Shrine]]
'''Shinjō Shrine''' (新城神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|神社}} located in {{ill|Iki City|en|壱岐市}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|長崎県}}, Japan.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|en|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|元寇}} and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|en|文永11年}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|en|元}} and their {{ill|Goryeo|en|高麗}} allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|en|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|en|樋詰城}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the {{ill|Battle of Bunei|en|文永の役}}. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|en|金方慶伝}} within the {{ill|History of Goryeo|en|高麗史}}:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * **1274 (Bunei 11):** Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * **1886 (Meiji 19):** Main hall construction.
== Annual Festivals == * **January 1:** New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * **February 11:** National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * **December 31:** New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * **Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō** (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * **Tōjinbaru Battlefield**: Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * **Koraibashi Battlefield**: Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * **Yuan Landing Site**: Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.
<gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Tōjinbaru Battlefield File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Koraibashi Battlefield File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|Landing site of Yuan forces File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|Scenic view of Urami Fishing Port </gallery>
== Access == ;Nearest Bus Stop * {{ill|Iki Kōtsū|en|壱岐交通}} "Shinjōbashi" Bus Stop ;Nearest Roads * {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture Route 23|en|長崎県道23号勝本石田線}} * 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ashibe Port|en|芦辺漁港}}
== References == * Shinjō Shrine Historical Record (新城神社御由緒書)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|元寇}} * {{ill|Iki Shrine|en|壱岐神社}} * {{ill|Iki City Museum|en|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} * {{ill|Komodahama Shrine|en|小茂田浜神社}} (Tsushima City) * {{ill|Shōni Suketoki|en|少弐資時}} * {{ill|Mongol Invasions Museum|en|元寇資料館}} (Fukuoka Prefecture)
== External Links == * [http://www.iki-brand.jp/info.php?bid=BA000128&key=新城神社&page_num=1 Shinjō Shrine Official Website]

{{Other uses|Shinjō Shrine in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|the shrine formerly located in Taiwan|Shinjō Shrine (Taiwan)}} {{Shrine |Name = Shinjō Shrine |Image = [[File:壱岐 新城神社鳥居.JPG|center|270px|Torii and main hall of Shinjō Shrine in Iki, the site of the Mongol invasions]] |Location = Shinjō Higashifure 441, Katsumoto Town, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 49 | Latitude seconds = 59.7 | N or S = N |Longitude degrees = 129 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 5.5 | E or W = E |Map country code = JP-42 |Map = Japan Nagasaki |Deity = Taira Kagetaka, soldiers who fell during the Mongol invasions, various other deities |Type = |Established = 1948 |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Sacred sites = |Annual festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|Interior of the main hall at Shinjō Shrine|200px]] [[File:Monument_of_Taira_Kagetaka's_Suicide_in_Shinjo_Shrine_on_Iki_Island.jpg|thumb|200px|Monument marking the site of Taira Kagetaka’s suicide within Shinjō Shrine]]
'''Shinjō Shrine''' (新城神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, Japan.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元}} and their {{ill|Goryeo|ja|高麗}} allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the {{ill|Battle of Bunei|ja|文永の役}}. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝}} within the {{ill|History of Goryeo|ja|高麗史}}:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * **1274 (Bunei 11):** Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * **1886 (Meiji 19):** Main hall construction.
== Annual Festivals == * **January 1:** New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * **February 11:** National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * **December 31:** New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * **Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō** (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * **Tōjinbaru Battlefield**: Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * **Koraibashi Battlefield**: Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * **Yuan Landing Site**: Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.
<gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Tōjinbaru Battlefield File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Koraibashi Battlefield File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|Landing site of Yuan forces File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|Scenic view of Urami Fishing Port </gallery>
== Access == ;Nearest Bus Stop * {{ill|Iki Kōtsū|ja|壱岐交通}} "Shinjōbashi" Bus Stop ;Nearest Roads * {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture Route 23|ja|長崎県道23号勝本石田線}} * 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ashibe Port|ja|芦辺漁港}}
== References == * Shinjō Shrine Historical Record (新城神社御由緒書)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} * {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐神社}} * {{ill|Iki City Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} * {{ill|Komodahama Shrine|ja|小茂田浜神社}} (Tsushima City) * {{ill|Shōni Suketoki|ja|少弐資時}} * {{ill|Mongol Invasions Museum|ja|元寇資料館}} (Fukuoka Prefecture)
== External Links == * [http://www.iki-brand.jp/info.php?bid=BA000128&key=新城神社&page_num=1 Shinjō Shrine Official Website]

{{Other uses|Shinjō Shrine in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|the shrine formerly located in Taiwan|Shinjō Shrine (Taiwan)}} {{Shrine |Name = Shinjō Shrine |Image = [[File:壱岐 新城神社鳥居.JPG|center|270px|Torii and main hall of Shinjō Shrine in Iki, the site of the Mongol invasions]] |Location = Shinjō Higashifure 441, Katsumoto Town, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 49 | Latitude seconds = 59.7 | N or S = N |Longitude degrees = 129 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 5.5 | E or W = E |Map country code = JP-42 |Map = Japan Nagasaki |Deity = Taira Kagetaka, soldiers who fell during the Mongol invasions, various other deities |Type = |Established = 1948 |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Sacred sites = |Annual festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|Interior of the main hall at Shinjō Shrine|200px]] [[File:Monument_of_Taira_Kagetaka's_Suicide_in_Shinjo_Shrine_on_Iki_Island.jpg|thumb|200px|Monument marking the site of Taira Kagetaka’s suicide within Shinjō Shrine]]
'''Shinjō Shrine''' (新城神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, Japan.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元}} and their {{ill|Goryeo|ja|高麗}} allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the {{ill|Battle of Bunei|ja|文永の役}}. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝}} within the {{ill|History of Goryeo|ja|高麗史}}:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== Annual Festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.
<gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Tōjinbaru Battlefield File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Koraibashi Battlefield File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|Landing site of Yuan forces File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|Scenic view of Urami Fishing Port </gallery>
== Access == ;Nearest Bus Stop * {{ill|Iki Kōtsū|ja|壱岐交通}} "Shinjōbashi" Bus Stop ;Nearest Roads * {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture Route 23|ja|長崎県道23号勝本石田線}} * 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ashibe Port|ja|芦辺漁港}}
== References == * Shinjō Shrine Historical Record (新城神社御由緒書)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} * {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐神社}} * {{ill|Iki City Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} * {{ill|Komodahama Shrine|ja|小茂田浜神社}} (Tsushima City) * {{ill|Shōni Suketoki|ja|少弐資時}} * {{ill|Mongol Invasions Museum|ja|元寇資料館}} (Fukuoka Prefecture)
== External Links == * [http://www.iki-brand.jp/info.php?bid=BA000128&key=新城神社&page_num=1 Shinjō Shrine Official Website]

{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.

'''Shinjō Shrine''' (新城神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, Japan.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元}} and their {{ill|Goryeo|ja|高麗}} allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the {{ill|Battle of Bunei|ja|文永の役}}. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝}} within the {{ill|History of Goryeo|ja|高麗史}}:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== Annual Festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.
<gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Tōjinbaru Battlefield File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Koraibashi Battlefield File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|Landing site of Yuan forces File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|Scenic view of Urami Fishing Port </gallery>

{{Other uses|Shinjō Shrine in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|the shrine formerly located in Taiwan|Shinjō Shrine (Taiwan)}} {{Shrine |Name = Shinjō Shrine |Image = [[File:壱岐 新城神社鳥居.JPG|center|270px|Torii and main hall of Shinjō Shrine in Iki, the site of the Mongol invasions]] |Location = Shinjō Higashifure 441, Katsumoto Town, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 49 | Latitude seconds = 59.7 | N or S = N |Longitude degrees = 129 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 5.5 | E or W = E |Map country code = JP-42 |Map = Japan Nagasaki |Deity = Taira Kagetaka, soldiers who fell during the Mongol invasions, various other deities |Type = |Established = 1948 |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Sacred sites = |Annual festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|Interior of the main hall at Shinjō Shrine|200px]] [[File:Monument_of_Taira_Kagetaka's_Suicide_in_Shinjo_Shrine_on_Iki_Island.jpg|thumb|200px|Monument marking the site of Taira Kagetaka’s suicide within Shinjō Shrine]]
'''Shinjō Shrine''' (新城神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, Japan.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元}} and their {{ill|Goryeo|ja|高麗}} allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the {{ill|Battle of Bunei|ja|文永の役}}. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝}} within the {{ill|History of Goryeo|ja|高麗史}}:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== Annual Festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.
<gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Tōjinbaru Battlefield File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Koraibashi Battlefield File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|Landing site of Yuan forces File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|Scenic view of Urami Fishing Port </gallery>

It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝}} within the {{ill|History of Goryeo|ja|高麗史}}:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.

{{Other uses|Shinjō Shrine in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|the shrine formerly located in Taiwan|Shinjō Shrine (Taiwan)}} {{Shrine |Name = Shinjō Shrine |image = [[File:壱岐 新城神社鳥居.JPG|center|270px|Torii and main hall of Shinjō Shrine in Iki, the site of the Mongol invasions]] |location = Shinjō Higashifure 441, Katsumoto Town, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 49 | Latitude seconds = 59.7 | N or S = N |Longitude degrees = 129 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 5.5 | E or W = E |Map country code = JP-42 |Map = Japan Nagasaki |deity = Taira Kagetaka, soldiers who fell during the Mongol invasions, various other deities |Type = |Established = 1948 |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Sacred sites = |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|Interior of the main hall at Shinjō Shrine|200px]] [[File:Monument_of_Taira_Kagetaka's_Suicide_in_Shinjo_Shrine_on_Iki_Island.jpg|thumb|200px|Monument marking the site of Taira Kagetaka’s suicide within Shinjō Shrine]]
'''Shinjō Shrine''' (新城神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, Japan.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元}} and their {{ill|Goryeo|ja|高麗}} allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the {{ill|Battle of Bunei|ja|文永の役}}. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝}} within the {{ill|History of Goryeo|ja|高麗史}}:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== Annual Festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.

{{Other uses|Shinjō Shrine in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|the shrine formerly located in Taiwan|Shinjō Shrine (Taiwan)}} {{Shrine |Name = Shinjō Shrine |image = [[File:壱岐 新城神社鳥居.JPG|center|270px|Torii and main hall of Shinjō Shrine in Iki, the site of the Mongol invasions]] |location = Shinjō Higashifure 441, Katsumoto Town, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 49 | Latitude seconds = 59.7 | N or S = N |Longitude degrees = 129 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 5.5 | E or W = E |Map country code = JP-42 |Map = Japan Nagasaki |deity = Taira Kagetaka, soldiers who fell during the Mongol invasions, various other deities |Type = |Established = 1948 |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Sacred sites = |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|Interior of the main hall at Shinjō Shrine|200px]] [[File:Monument_of_Taira_Kagetaka's_Suicide_in_Shinjo_Shrine_on_Iki_Island.jpg|thumb|200px|Monument marking the site of Taira Kagetaka’s suicide within Shinjō Shrine]]
'''Shinjō Shrine''' (新城神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, Japan.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元}} and their {{ill|Goryeo|ja|高麗}} allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the {{ill|Battle of Bunei|ja|文永の役}}. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝}} within the {{ill|History of Goryeo|ja|高麗史}}:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== Annual Festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.
<gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Tōjinbaru Battlefield File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Koraibashi Battlefield File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|Landing site of Yuan forces File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|Scenic view of Urami Fishing Port </gallery>

'''Shinjō Shrine''' (新城神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, Japan.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元}} and their {{ill|Goryeo|ja|高麗}} allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the {{ill|Battle of Bunei|ja|文永の役}}. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝}} within the {{ill|History of Goryeo|ja|高麗史}}:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== Annual Festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.
<gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Tōjinbaru Battlefield File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Koraibashi Battlefield File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|Landing site of Yuan forces File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|Scenic view of Urami Fishing Port </gallery>

{{Other uses|Shinjō Shrine in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|the shrine formerly located in Taiwan|Shinjō Shrine (Taiwan)}} {{Shrine |Name = Shinjō Shrine |image = [[File:壱岐 新城神社鳥居.JPG|center|270px|Torii and main hall of Shinjō Shrine in Iki, the site of the Mongol invasions]] |location = Shinjō Higashifure 441, Katsumoto Town, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 49 | Latitude seconds = 59.7 | N or S = N |Longitude degrees = 129 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 5.5 | E or W = E |Map country code = JP-42 |Map = Japan Nagasaki |deity = Taira Kagetaka, soldiers who fell during the Mongol invasions, various other deities |Type = |Established = 1948 |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Sacred sites = |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|Interior of the main hall at Shinjō Shrine|200px]] [[File:Monument_of_Taira_Kagetaka's_Suicide_in_Shinjo_Shrine_on_Iki_Island.jpg|thumb|200px|Monument marking the site of Taira Kagetaka’s suicide within Shinjō Shrine]]
'''Shinjō Shrine''' (新城神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, Japan.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元}} and their {{ill|Goryeo|ja|高麗}} allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the {{ill|Battle of Bunei|ja|文永の役}}. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝}} within the {{ill|Goryeosa|ja|高麗史}}:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== Annual Festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.
<gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Tōjinbaru Battlefield File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Koraibashi Battlefield File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|Landing site of Yuan forces File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|Scenic view of Urami Fishing Port </gallery>

{{Other uses|Shinjō Shrine in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|the shrine formerly located in Taiwan|Shinjō Shrine (Taiwan)}} {{Shrine |Name = Shinjō Shrine |image = [[File:壱岐 新城神社鳥居.JPG|center|270px|Torii and main hall of Shinjō Shrine in Iki, the site of the Mongol invasions]] |location = Shinjō Higashifure 441, Katsumoto Town, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 49 | Latitude seconds = 59.7 | N or S = N |Longitude degrees = 129 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 5.5 | E or W = E |Map country code = JP-42 |Map = Japan Nagasaki |deity = Taira Kagetaka, soldiers who fell during the Mongol invasions, various other deities |Type = |Established = 1948 |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Sacred sites = |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|Interior of the main hall at Shinjō Shrine|200px]] [[File:Monument_of_Taira_Kagetaka's_Suicide_in_Shinjo_Shrine_on_Iki_Island.jpg|thumb|200px|Monument marking the site of Taira Kagetaka’s suicide within Shinjō Shrine]]
'''Shinjō Shrine''' (新城神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, Japan.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元}} and their {{ill|Goryeo|ja|高麗}} allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the {{ill|Battle of Bunei|ja|文永の役}}. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝}} within the {{ill|Goryeosa|ja|高麗史}}:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== Annual Festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.
<gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Tōjinbaru Battlefield File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Koraibashi Battlefield File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|Landing site of Yuan forces File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|Scenic view of Urami Fishing Port </gallery>

{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.

revision.text:
{{Other uses|Shinjō Shrine in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|the shrine formerly located in Taiwan|Shinjō Shrine (Taiwan)}} {{Shrine |name = Shinjō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:壱岐 新城神社鳥居.JPG|center|270px|Torii and main hall of Shinjō Shrine in Iki, the site of the Mongol invasions]] |location = Shinjō Higashifure 441, Katsumoto Town, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 49 | Latitude seconds = 59.7 | N or S = N |Longitude degrees = 129 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 5.5 | E or W = E |Map country code = JP-42 |Map = Japan Nagasaki |deity = Taira Kagetaka, soldiers who fell during the Mongol invasions, various other deities |Type = |Established = 1948 |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Sacred sites = |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|Interior of the main hall at Shinjō Shrine|200px]] [[File:Monument_of_Taira_Kagetaka's_Suicide_in_Shinjo_Shrine_on_Iki_Island.jpg|thumb|200px|Monument marking the site of Taira Kagetaka’s suicide within Shinjō Shrine]]
'''Shinjō Shrine''' (新城神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, Japan.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元}} and their {{ill|Goryeo|ja|高麗}} allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the {{ill|Battle of Bunei|ja|文永の役}}. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝}} within the {{ill|Goryeosa|ja|高麗史}}:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.
<gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Tōjinbaru Battlefield File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Koraibashi Battlefield File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|Landing site of Yuan forces File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|Scenic view of Urami Fishing Port </gallery>
== Access == ;Nearest Bus Stop * {{ill|Iki Kōtsū|ja|壱岐交通}} "Shinjōbashi" Bus Stop ;Nearest Roads * {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture Route 23|ja|長崎県道23号勝本石田線}} * 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ashibe Port|ja|芦辺漁港}}
== References == * Shinjō Shrine Historical Record (新城神社御由緒書)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} * {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐神社}} * {{ill|Iki City Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} * {{ill|Komodahama Shrine|ja|小茂田浜神社}} (Tsushima City) * {{ill|Shōni Suketoki|ja|少弐資時}} * {{ill|Mongol Invasions Museum|ja|元寇資料館}} (Fukuoka Prefecture)
== External Links == * [http://www.iki-brand.jp/info.php?bid=BA000128&key=新城神社&page_num=1 Shinjō Shrine Official Website]

revision.text:
{{Other uses|Shinjō Shrine in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|the shrine formerly located in Taiwan|Shinjō Shrine (Taiwan)}} {{Shrine |name = Shinjō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:壱岐 新城神社鳥居.JPG|center|270px|Torii and main hall of Shinjō Shrine in Iki, the site of the Mongol invasions]] |location = Shinjō Higashifure 441, Katsumoto Town, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 49 | Latitude seconds = 59.7 | N or S = N |Longitude degrees = 129 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 5.5 | E or W = E |Map country code = JP-42 |Map = Japan Nagasaki |deity = Taira Kagetaka, soldiers who fell during the Mongol invasions, various other deities |Type = |Established = 1948 |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Sacred sites = |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|Interior of the main hall at Shinjō Shrine|200px]] [[File:Monument_of_Taira_Kagetaka's_Suicide_in_Shinjo_Shrine_on_Iki_Island.jpg|thumb|200px|Monument marking the site of Taira Kagetaka’s suicide within Shinjō Shrine]]
'''Shinjō Shrine''' (新城神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, Japan.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元}} and their {{ill|Goryeo|ja|高麗}} allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the {{ill|Battle of Bunei|ja|文永の役}}. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝}} within the {{ill|Goryeosa|ja|高麗史}}:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.
<gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Tōjinbaru Battlefield File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Koraibashi Battlefield File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|Landing site of Yuan forces File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|Scenic view of Urami Fishing Port </gallery>
== Access == ;Nearest Bus Stop * {{ill|Iki Kōtsū|ja|壱岐交通}} "Shinjōbashi" Bus Stop ;Nearest Roads * {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture Route 23|ja|長崎県道23号勝本石田線}} * 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ashibe Port|ja|芦辺漁港}}
== References == * Shinjō Shrine Historical Record (新城神社御由緒書)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} * {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐神社}} * {{ill|Iki City Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} * {{ill|Komodahama Shrine|ja|小茂田浜神社}} (Tsushima City) * {{ill|Shōni Suketoki|ja|少弐資時}} * {{ill|Mongol Invasions Museum|ja|元寇資料館}} (Fukuoka Prefecture)

{{Other uses|Shinjō Shrine in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|the shrine formerly located in Taiwan|Shinjō Shrine (Taiwan)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shinjō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:壱岐 新城神社鳥居.JPG|center|270px|Torii and main hall of Shinjō Shrine in Iki, the site of the Mongol invasions]] |location = Shinjō Higashifure 441, Katsumoto Town, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 49 | Latitude seconds = 59.7 | N or S = N |Longitude degrees = 129 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 5.5 | E or W = E |Map country code = JP-42 |Map = Japan Nagasaki |deity = Taira Kagetaka, soldiers who fell during the Mongol invasions, various other deities |Type = |Established = 1948 |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Sacred sites = |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|Interior of the main hall at Shinjō Shrine|200px]] [[File:Monument_of_Taira_Kagetaka's_Suicide_in_Shinjo_Shrine_on_Iki_Island.jpg|thumb|200px|Monument marking the site of Taira Kagetaka’s suicide within Shinjō Shrine]]
'''Shinjō Shrine''' (新城神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, Japan.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元}} and their {{ill|Goryeo|ja|高麗}} allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the {{ill|Battle of Bunei|ja|文永の役}}. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝}} within the {{ill|Goryeosa|ja|高麗史}}:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.

== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元}} and their {{ill|Goryeo|ja|高麗}} allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the {{ill|Battle of Bunei|ja|文永の役}}. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝}} within the {{ill|Goryeosa|ja|高麗史}}:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.
<gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Tōjinbaru Battlefield File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Koraibashi Battlefield File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|Landing site of Yuan forces File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|Scenic view of Urami Fishing Port </gallery>

== Related Articles == * {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}} * {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐神社}} * {{ill|Iki City Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} * {{ill|Komodahama Shrine|ja|小茂田浜神社}} (Tsushima City) * {{ill|Shōni Suketoki|ja|少弐資時}} * {{ill|Mongol Invasions Museum|ja|元寇資料館}} (Fukuoka Prefecture)
