{{Other uses|Shinjō Shrine in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|the shrine formerly located in Taiwan|Shinjō Shrine (Taiwan)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shinjō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:壱岐 新城神社鳥居.JPG|center|270px|Torii and main hall of Shinjō Shrine in Iki, the site of the Mongol invasions]] |location = Shinjō Higashifure 441, Katsumoto Town, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 49 | Latitude seconds = 59.7 | N or S = N |Longitude degrees = 129 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 5.5 | E or W = E |Map country code = JP-42 |Map = Japan Nagasaki |deity = Taira Kagetaka, soldiers who fell during the Mongol invasions, various other deities |Type = |Established = 1948 |Main hall = |alternate_name = |Sacred sites = |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|Interior of the main hall at Shinjō Shrine|200px]] [[File:Monument_of_Taira_Kagetaka's_Suicide_in_Shinjo_Shrine_on_Iki_Island.jpg|thumb|200px|Monument marking the site of Taira Kagetaka’s suicide within Shinjō Shrine]]
'''Shinjō Shrine''' (新城神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and their {{ill|Goryeo|ja|高麗|qq=draft|qq=draft}} allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the {{ill|Battle of Bunei|ja|文永の役|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the {{ill|Goryeosa|ja|高麗史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.
<gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Tōjinbaru Battlefield File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Koraibashi Battlefield File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|Landing site of Yuan forces File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|Scenic view of Urami Fishing Port </gallery>
== Access == ;Nearest Bus Stop * {{ill|Iki Kōtsū|ja|壱岐交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Shinjōbashi" Bus Stop ;Nearest Roads * {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture Route 23|ja|長崎県道23号勝本石田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ashibe Port|ja|芦辺漁港|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and their {{ill|Goryeo|ja|高麗|qq=draft|qq=draft}} allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the {{ill|Battle of Bunei|ja|文永の役|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the {{ill|Goryeosa|ja|高麗史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.
<gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Tōjinbaru Battlefield File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Koraibashi Battlefield File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|Landing site of Yuan forces File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|Scenic view of Urami Fishing Port </gallery>
== Access == ;Nearest Bus Stop * {{ill|Iki Kōtsū|ja|壱岐交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Shinjōbashi" Bus Stop ;Nearest Roads * {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture Route 23|ja|長崎県道23号勝本石田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ashibe Port|ja|芦辺漁港|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

== Related Articles == * {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Iki City Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Komodahama Shrine|ja|小茂田浜神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tsushima City) * {{ill|Shōni Suketoki|ja|少弐資時|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mongol Invasions Museum|ja|元寇資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Fukuoka Prefecture)

{{Other uses|Shinjō Shrine in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|the shrine formerly located in Taiwan|Shinjō Shrine (Taiwan)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shinjō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:壱岐 新城神社鳥居.JPG|center|270px|Torii and main hall of Shinjō Shrine in Iki, the site of the Mongol invasions]] |location = Shinjō Higashifure 441, Katsumoto Town, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 49 | Latitude seconds = 59.7 | N or S = N |Longitude degrees = 129 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 5.5 | E or W = E |Map country code = JP-42 |Map = Japan Nagasaki |deity = Taira Kagetaka, soldiers who fell during the Mongol invasions, various other deities |Type = |Established = 1948 |Main hall = |alternate_name = |Sacred sites = |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|Interior of the main hall at Shinjō Shrine|200px]] [[File:Monument_of_Taira_Kagetaka's_Suicide_in_Shinjo_Shrine_on_Iki_Island.jpg|thumb|200px|Monument marking the site of Taira Kagetaka’s suicide within Shinjō Shrine]]
'''Shinjō Shrine''' (新城神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira Kagetaka|qq=}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mongol invasions of Japan|qq=}} and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|qq=}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuan Dynasty|qq=}} and their {{ill|Goryeo|ja|高麗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryeo|qq=}} allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira Kagetaka|qq=}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizume Castle|qq=}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the {{ill|Battle of Bunei|ja|文永の役|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Bunei|qq=}}. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|qq=}} within the {{ill|Goryeosa|ja|高麗史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryeosa|qq=}}:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.
<gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Tōjinbaru Battlefield File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Koraibashi Battlefield File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|Landing site of Yuan forces File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|Scenic view of Urami Fishing Port </gallery>
== Access == ;Nearest Bus Stop * {{ill|Iki Kōtsū|ja|壱岐交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kōtsū|qq=}} "Shinjōbashi" Bus Stop ;Nearest Roads * {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture Route 23|ja|長崎県道23号勝本石田線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture Route 23|qq=}} * 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ashibe Port|ja|芦辺漁港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashibe Port|qq=}}
== References == * Shinjō Shrine Historical Record (新城神社御由緒書)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mongol invasions of Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Iki City Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Museum|qq=}} * {{ill|Komodahama Shrine|ja|小茂田浜神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komodahama Shrine|qq=}} (Tsushima City) * {{ill|Shōni Suketoki|ja|少弐資時|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Suketoki|qq=}} * {{ill|Mongol Invasions Museum|ja|元寇資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mongol Invasions Museum|qq=}} (Fukuoka Prefecture)

{{Other uses|Shinjō Shrine in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|the shrine formerly located in Taiwan|Shinjō Shrine (Taiwan)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shinjō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:壱岐 新城神社鳥居.JPG|center|270px|Torii and main hall of Shinjō Shrine in Iki, the site of the Mongol invasions]] |location = Shinjō Higashifure 441, Katsumoto Town, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 49 | Latitude seconds = 59.7 | N or S = N |Longitude degrees = 129 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 5.5 | E or W = E |Map country code = JP-42 |Map = Japan Nagasaki |deity = Taira Kagetaka, soldiers who fell during the Mongol invasions, various other deities |Type = |Established = 1948 |Main hall = |alternate_name = |Sacred sites = |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|Interior of the main hall at Shinjō Shrine|200px]] [[File:Monument_of_Taira_Kagetaka's_Suicide_in_Shinjo_Shrine_on_Iki_Island.jpg|thumb|200px|Monument marking the site of Taira Kagetaka’s suicide within Shinjō Shrine]]
'''Shinjō Shrine''' (新城神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], Japan.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira Kagetaka|qq=}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|qq=}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuan Dynasty|qq=}} and their [[:en:Goryeo|Goryeo]] allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira Kagetaka|qq=}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizume Castle|qq=}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bunei]]. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|qq=}} within the [[:en:Goryeosa|Goryeosa]]:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}

{{Other uses|Shinjō Shrine in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|the shrine formerly located in Taiwan|Shinjō Shrine (Taiwan)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shinjō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:壱岐 新城神社鳥居.JPG|center|270px|Torii and main hall of Shinjō Shrine in Iki, the site of the Mongol invasions]] |location = Shinjō Higashifure 441, Katsumoto Town, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 49 | Latitude seconds = 59.7 | N or S = N |Longitude degrees = 129 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 5.5 | E or W = E |Map country code = JP-42 |Map = Japan Nagasaki |deity = Taira Kagetaka, soldiers who fell during the Mongol invasions, various other deities |Type = |Established = 1948 |Main hall = |alternate_name = |Sacred sites = |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|Interior of the main hall at Shinjō Shrine|200px]] [[File:Monument_of_Taira_Kagetaka's_Suicide_in_Shinjo_Shrine_on_Iki_Island.jpg|thumb|200px|Monument marking the site of Taira Kagetaka’s suicide within Shinjō Shrine]]
'''Shinjō Shrine''' (新城神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], Japan.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira Kagetaka|qq=}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|qq=}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuan Dynasty|qq=}} and their [[:en:Goryeo|Goryeo]] allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira Kagetaka|qq=}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizume Castle|qq=}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bunei]]. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|qq=}} within the [[:en:Goryeosa|Goryeosa]]:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.
<gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Tōjinbaru Battlefield File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Koraibashi Battlefield File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|Landing site of Yuan forces File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|Scenic view of Urami Fishing Port </gallery>
== Access == ;Nearest Bus Stop * {{ill|Iki Kōtsū|ja|壱岐交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kōtsū|qq=}} "Shinjōbashi" Bus Stop ;Nearest Roads * {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture Route 23|ja|長崎県道23号勝本石田線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture Route 23|qq=}} * 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ashibe Port|ja|芦辺漁港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashibe Port|qq=}}
== References == * Shinjō Shrine Historical Record (新城神社御由緒書)
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] * {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Iki City Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Museum|qq=}} * {{ill|Komodahama Shrine|ja|小茂田浜神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komodahama Shrine|qq=}} (Tsushima City) * {{ill|Shōni Suketoki|ja|少弐資時|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Suketoki|qq=}} * {{ill|Mongol Invasions Museum|ja|元寇資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mongol Invasions Museum|qq=}} (Fukuoka Prefecture)

revision.text:
{{Other uses|Shinjō Shrine in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|the shrine formerly located in Taiwan|Shinjō Shrine (Taiwan)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shinjō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:壱岐 新城神社鳥居.JPG|center|270px|Torii and main hall of Shinjō Shrine in Iki, the site of the Mongol invasions]] |location = Shinjō Higashifure 441, Katsumoto Town, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 49 | Latitude seconds = 59.7 | N or S = N |Longitude degrees = 129 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 5.5 | E or W = E |Map country code = JP-42 |Map = Japan Nagasaki |deity = Taira Kagetaka, soldiers who fell during the Mongol invasions, various other deities |Type = |Established = 1948 |Main hall = |alternate_name = |Sacred sites = |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|Interior of the main hall at Shinjō Shrine|200px]] [[File:Monument_of_Taira_Kagetaka's_Suicide_in_Shinjo_Shrine_on_Iki_Island.jpg|thumb|200px|Monument marking the site of Taira Kagetaka’s suicide within Shinjō Shrine]]
'''Shinjō Shrine''' (新城神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], Japan.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira Kagetaka|qq=}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|qq=}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuan Dynasty|qq=}} and their [[:en:Goryeo|Goryeo]] allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira Kagetaka|qq=}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizume Castle|qq=}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bunei]]. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|qq=}} within the [[:en:Goryeosa|Goryeosa]]:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.
<gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Tōjinbaru Battlefield File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Koraibashi Battlefield File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|Landing site of Yuan forces File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|Scenic view of Urami Fishing Port </gallery>
== Access == ;Nearest Bus Stop * {{ill|Iki Kōtsū|ja|壱岐交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kōtsū|qq=}} "Shinjōbashi" Bus Stop ;Nearest Roads * {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture Route 23|ja|長崎県道23号勝本石田線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture Route 23|qq=}} * 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ashibe Port|ja|芦辺漁港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashibe Port|qq=}}
== References == * Shinjō Shrine Historical Record (新城神社御由緒書)
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] * {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Iki City Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Museum|qq=}} * {{ill|Komodahama Shrine|ja|小茂田浜神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komodahama Shrine|qq=}} (Tsushima City) * {{ill|Shōni Suketoki|ja|少弐資時|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Suketoki|qq=}} * {{ill|Mongol Invasions Museum|ja|元寇資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mongol Invasions Museum|qq=}} (Fukuoka Prefecture)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shinjō-jinja (Iki)]][[ja:新城神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17993923]] Wikidata name: Shinjō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Other uses|Shinjō Shrine in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|the shrine formerly located in Taiwan|Shinjō Shrine (Taiwan)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shinjō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:壱岐 新城神社鳥居.JPG |location = Shinjō Higashifure 441, Katsumoto Town, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 49 | Latitude seconds = 59.7 | N or S = N |Longitude degrees = 129 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 5.5 | E or W = E |Map country code = JP-42 |Map = Japan Nagasaki |deity = {{ill|Q10281419|qid=Q10281419}} |Type = |Established = 1948 |Main hall = |alternate_name = |Sacred sites = |festival = |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Iki|qid=Q861519}} |coordinates = {{Coord|33.83325|N|129.718194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|Interior of the main hall at Shinjō Shrine|200px]] [[File:Monument_of_Taira_Kagetaka's_Suicide_in_Shinjo_Shrine_on_Iki_Island.jpg|thumb|200px|Monument marking the site of Taira Kagetaka’s suicide within Shinjō Shrine]]
'''Shinjō Shrine''' (新城神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], Japan.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira Kagetaka|qq=|1=Taira Kagetaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10281419}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuan Dynasty|qq=|1=元|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22918383}} and their [[:en:Goryeo|Goryeo]] allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira Kagetaka|qq=|1=Taira Kagetaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10281419}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizume Castle|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bunei]]. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the [[:en:Goryeosa|Goryeosa]]:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.
<gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Tōjinbaru Battlefield File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Koraibashi Battlefield File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|Landing site of Yuan forces File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|Scenic view of Urami Fishing Port </gallery>
== Access == ;Nearest Bus Stop * {{ill|Iki Kōtsū|ja|壱岐交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kōtsū|qq=|1=Iki Kōtsū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429582}} "Shinjōbashi" Bus Stop ;Nearest Roads * {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture Route 23|ja|長崎県道23号勝本石田線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture Route 23|qq=|1=Nagasaki Prefectural Road Route 23|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11652826}} * 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ashibe Port|ja|芦辺漁港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashibe Port|qq=|1=Ashibe Fishing Port|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11614922}}

== Related Articles == * [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] * {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Iki City Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Museum|qq=|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429588}} * {{ill|Komodahama Shrine|ja|小茂田浜神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komodahama Shrine|qq=|1=Komodahama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991450}} (Tsushima City) * {{ill|Shōni Suketoki|ja|少弐資時|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Suketoki|qq=}} * {{ill|Mongol Invasions Museum|ja|元寇資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mongol Invasions Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元寇史料館|1=Genkō Historical Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10889644}} (Fukuoka Prefecture)

'''Shinjō Shrine''' (新城神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], Japan.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira Kagetaka|qq=|1=Taira Kagetaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10281419}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuan Dynasty|qq=|1=元|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22918383}} and their [[:en:Goryeo|Goryeo]] allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira Kagetaka|qq=|1=Taira Kagetaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10281419}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizume Castle|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bunei]]. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the [[:en:Goryeosa|Goryeosa]]:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.
<gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Tōjinbaru Battlefield File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Koraibashi Battlefield File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|Landing site of Yuan forces File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|Scenic view of Urami Fishing Port </gallery>
== Access == ;Nearest Bus Stop * {{ill|Iki Kōtsū|ja|壱岐交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kōtsū|qq=|1=Iki Kōtsū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429582}} "Shinjōbashi" Bus Stop ;Nearest Roads * {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture Route 23|ja|長崎県道23号勝本石田線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture Route 23|qq=|1=Nagasaki Prefectural Road Route 23|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11652826}} * 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ashibe Port|ja|芦辺漁港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashibe Port|qq=|1=Ashibe Fishing Port|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11614922}}
== References == * Shinjō Shrine Historical Record (新城神社御由緒書)
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] * {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Iki City Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Museum|qq=|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429588}} * {{ill|Komodahama Shrine|ja|小茂田浜神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komodahama Shrine|qq=|1=Komodahama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991450}} (Tsushima City) * {{ill|Shōni Suketoki|ja|少弐資時|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Suketoki|qq=}} * {{ill|Mongol Invasions Museum|ja|元寇資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mongol Invasions Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元寇史料館|1=Genkō Historical Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10889644}} (Fukuoka Prefecture)
== External Links == * [http://www.iki-brand.jp/info.php?bid=BA000128&key=新城神社&page_num=1 Shinjō Shrine Official Website]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shinjō-jinja (Iki)]][[ja:新城神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17993923]] Wikidata name: Shinjō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Other uses|Shinjō Shrine in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|the shrine formerly located in Taiwan|Shinjō Shrine (Taiwan)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shinjō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:壱岐 新城神社鳥居.JPG |location = Shinjō Higashifure 441, Katsumoto Town, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 49 | Latitude seconds = 59.7 | N or S = N |Longitude degrees = 129 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 5.5 | E or W = E |Map country code = JP-42 |Map = Japan Nagasaki |deity = {{ill|Q10281419|WD=Q10281419}} |Type = |Established = 1948 |Main hall = |alternate_name = |Sacred sites = |festival = |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Iki|WD=Q861519}} |coordinates = {{Coord|33.83325|N|129.718194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|Interior of the main hall at Shinjō Shrine|200px]] [[File:Monument_of_Taira_Kagetaka's_Suicide_in_Shinjo_Shrine_on_Iki_Island.jpg|thumb|200px|Monument marking the site of Taira Kagetaka’s suicide within Shinjō Shrine]]
'''Shinjō Shrine''' (新城神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], Japan.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira Kagetaka|1=Taira Kagetaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10281419}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuan Dynasty|1=元|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22918383}} and their [[:en:Goryeo|Goryeo]] allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira Kagetaka|1=Taira Kagetaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10281419}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizume Castle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bunei]]. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the [[:en:Goryeosa|Goryeosa]]:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.
<gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Tōjinbaru Battlefield File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Koraibashi Battlefield File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|Landing site of Yuan forces File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|Scenic view of Urami Fishing Port </gallery>

'''Shinjō Shrine''' (新城神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], Japan.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira Kagetaka|1=Taira Kagetaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10281419}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuan Dynasty|1=元|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22918383}} and their [[:en:Goryeo|Goryeo]] allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira Kagetaka|1=Taira Kagetaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10281419}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizume Castle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bunei]]. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the [[:en:Goryeosa|Goryeosa]]:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.
<gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Tōjinbaru Battlefield File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Koraibashi Battlefield File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|Landing site of Yuan forces File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|Scenic view of Urami Fishing Port </gallery>
== Access == ;Nearest Bus Stop * {{ill|Iki Kōtsū|ja|壱岐交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kōtsū|1=Iki Kōtsū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429582}} "Shinjōbashi" Bus Stop ;Nearest Roads * {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture Route 23|ja|長崎県道23号勝本石田線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture Route 23|1=Nagasaki Prefectural Road Route 23|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11652826}} * 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ashibe Port|ja|芦辺漁港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashibe Port|1=Ashibe Fishing Port|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11614922}}
== References == * Shinjō Shrine Historical Record (新城神社御由緒書)
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] * {{ill|Iki Gokoku Shrine|ja|壱岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Iki City Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Museum|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429588}} * {{ill|Komodahama Shrine|ja|小茂田浜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komodahama Shrine|1=Komodahama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991450}} (Tsushima City) * {{ill|Shōni Suketoki|ja|少弐資時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōni Suketoki|qq=}} * {{ill|Mongol Invasions Museum|ja|元寇資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mongol Invasions Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元寇史料館|1=Genkō Historical Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10889644}} (Fukuoka Prefecture)
== External Links == * [http://www.iki-brand.jp/info.php?bid=BA000128&key=新城神社&page_num=1 Shinjō Shrine Official Website]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shinjō-jinja (Iki)]][[ja:新城神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17993923]] Wikidata name: Shinjō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Other uses|Shinjō Shrine in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|the shrine formerly located in Taiwan|Shinjō Shrine (Taiwan)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shinjō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:壱岐 新城神社鳥居.JPG |location = Shinjō Higashifure 441, Katsumoto Town, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 49 | Latitude seconds = 59.7 | N or S = N |Longitude degrees = 129 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 5.5 | E or W = E |Map country code = JP-42 |Map = Japan Nagasaki |deity = {{ill|Q10281419|WD=Q10281419}} |Type = |Established = 1948 |Main hall = |alternate_name = |Sacred sites = |festival = |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Iki|WD=Q861519}} |coordinates = {{Coord|33.83325|N|129.718194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|Interior of the main hall at Shinjō Shrine|200px]] [[File:Monument_of_Taira_Kagetaka's_Suicide_in_Shinjo_Shrine_on_Iki_Island.jpg|thumb|200px|Monument marking the site of Taira Kagetaka’s suicide within Shinjō Shrine]]
'''Shinjō Shrine''' (新城神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], Japan.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira Kagetaka|1=Taira Kagetaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10281419}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuan Dynasty|1=元|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22918383}} and their [[:en:Goryeo|Goryeo]] allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira Kagetaka|1=Taira Kagetaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10281419}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizume Castle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bunei]]. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the [[:en:Goryeosa|Goryeosa]]:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.
<gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Tōjinbaru Battlefield File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Koraibashi Battlefield File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|Landing site of Yuan forces File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|Scenic view of Urami Fishing Port </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shinjō-jinja (Iki)]][[ja:新城神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17993923]] Wikidata name: Shinjō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Other uses|Shinjō Shrine in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|the shrine formerly located in Taiwan|Shinjō Shrine (Taiwan)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shinjō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:壱岐 新城神社鳥居.JPG |location = Shinjō Higashifure 441, Katsumoto Town, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 49 | Latitude seconds = 59.7 | N or S = N |Longitude degrees = 129 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 5.5 | E or W = E |Map country code = JP-42 |Map = Japan Nagasaki |deity = {{ill|Q10281419|WD=Q10281419}} |Type = |Established = 1948 |Main hall = |alternate_name = |Sacred sites = |festival = |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Iki|WD=Q861519}} |coordinates = {{Coord|33.83325|N|129.718194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|Interior of the main hall at Shinjō Shrine|200px]] [[File:Monument_of_Taira_Kagetaka's_Suicide_in_Shinjo_Shrine_on_Iki_Island.jpg|thumb|200px|Monument marking the site of Taira Kagetaka’s suicide within Shinjō Shrine]]
'''Shinjō Shrine''' (新城神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], Japan.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira Kagetaka|1=Taira Kagetaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10281419}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuan Dynasty|1=元|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22918383}} and their [[:en:Goryeo|Goryeo]] allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira Kagetaka|1=Taira Kagetaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10281419}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizume Castle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bunei]]. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the [[:en:Goryeosa|Goryeosa]]:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.
<gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Tōjinbaru Battlefield File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Koraibashi Battlefield File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|Landing site of Yuan forces File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|Scenic view of Urami Fishing Port </gallery>

A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the [[:en:Goryeosa|Goryeosa]]:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.
<gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Tōjinbaru Battlefield File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Koraibashi Battlefield File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|Landing site of Yuan forces File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|Scenic view of Urami Fishing Port </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shinjō-jinja (Iki)]][[ja:新城神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17993923]] Wikidata name: Shinjō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Other uses|Shinjō Shrine in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|the shrine formerly located in Taiwan|Shinjō Shrine (Taiwan)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shinjō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:壱岐 新城神社鳥居.JPG |location = Shinjō Higashifure 441, Katsumoto Town, Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 49 | Latitude seconds = 59.7 | N or S = N |Longitude degrees = 129 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 5.5 | E or W = E |Map country code = JP-42 |Map = Japan Nagasaki |deity = {{ill|Q10281419|WD=Q10281419}} |Type = |Established = 1948 |Main hall = |alternate_name = |Sacred sites = |festival = |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Iki|WD=Q861519}} |coordinates = {{Coord|33.83325|N|129.718194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:壱岐 新城神社神殿内部.JPG|thumb|Interior of the main hall at Shinjō Shrine|200px]] [[File:Monument_of_Taira_Kagetaka's_Suicide_in_Shinjo_Shrine_on_Iki_Island.jpg|thumb|200px|Monument marking the site of Taira Kagetaka’s suicide within Shinjō Shrine]]
'''Shinjō Shrine''' (新城神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], Japan.
== Deities == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira Kagetaka|1=Taira Kagetaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10281419}}, the guardian of Iki, alongside soldiers who perished in the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] and other revered deities.
== Overview == On October 14, 1274 ({{ill|Bunei 11|ja|文永11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), forces of the {{ill|Yuan Dynasty|ja|元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuan Dynasty|1=元|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22918383}} and their [[:en:Goryeo|Goryeo]] allies landed on Iki Island, following their attack on Tsushima. {{ill|Taira Kagetaka|ja|平景隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira Kagetaka|1=Taira Kagetaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10281419}}, the guardian of Iki, led approximately 100 horsemen in a valiant defense. Despite their efforts, they were overwhelmed by the invading forces. On October 15, Taira Kagetaka took his own life at {{ill|Hizume Castle|ja|樋詰城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizume Castle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Shinjō Shrine is situated on the former site of Hizume Castle, the final stronghold of Taira Kagetaka during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bunei]]. The shrine honors his sacrifice in protecting Japan during this national crisis. It also serves as a memorial for the spirits of those who perished, embodying their protection over the nation during times of both conflict and peace.
The islanders of Iki expressed their wish to establish Shinjō Shrine as early as 1274. Their vision was partially realized in 1886 with the construction of the main hall.
A description of the battle at Iki is recorded in the {{ill|Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja|金方慶伝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Biography of Kim Bang-gyeong|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the [[:en:Goryeosa|Goryeosa]]:
{{quotation|When the Yuan forces reached Iki Island, the Japanese troops had formed a defensive line on the coast. The Goryeo generals, Park Ji-ryang and Zhao Bian, defeated them and pursued the fleeing Japanese soldiers. The Japanese forces on Iki surrendered but lost over 1,000 troops.}}
== History == * '''1274 (Bunei 11):''' Massacre of Iki residents during the Mongol-Goryeo invasion. * '''1886 (Meiji 19):''' Main hall construction.
== festivals == * '''January 1:''' New Year’s Festival (Gantan-sai) * '''February 11:''' National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkoku Kinen-sai) * '''December 31:''' New Year’s Eve Festival (Joya-sai)
== Nearby Historical Sites == * '''Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō''' (Shinjō Kosenjō): Located at Katsumoto Town, Shinjō Higashifure 1187<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1093 Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō (Iki City Cultural Information)] - Iki City Museum of Iki Fudoki No Oka</ref> * '''Tōjinbaru Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Hokushifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1127 Tōjinbaru Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Koraibashi Battlefield''': Located in Katsumoto Town, Nishifure<ref>[http://www.iki-haku.jp/museumInet/ikf/poiGet.do?id=1126 Koraibashi Battlefield Information]</ref> * '''Yuan Landing Site''': Located at Urami Fishing Port, Katsumoto Town.
<gallery> File:Site_of_Shinjo_Battlefileld_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Bunei Battlefield at Shinjō File:Site_of_Tojinbaru_Battlefiled_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Tōjinbaru Battlefield File:Site_of_Koraibashi_Battlefield_on_Iki_Island_on_First_Mongol_Invasion_of_Japan_in_1274.jpg|Koraibashi Battlefield File:Landing Place in Iki when Mongol invaded Japan in 1274.jpg|Landing site of Yuan forces File:Uromi Fishing Port in Katsumoto, Iki, Nagasaki.jpg|Scenic view of Urami Fishing Port </gallery>
== Access == ;Nearest Bus Stop * {{ill|Iki Kōtsū|ja|壱岐交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kōtsū|1=Iki Kōtsū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429582}} "Shinjōbashi" Bus Stop ;Nearest Roads * {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture Route 23|ja|長崎県道23号勝本石田線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture Route 23|1=Nagasaki Prefectural Road Route 23|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11652826}} * 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ashibe Port|ja|芦辺漁港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashibe Port|1=Ashibe Fishing Port|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11614922}}
